channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Why US Supreme Court Birthright Citizenship Ruling Is A Relief For Indians

media:content.media:description: In the late 1800s, birthright citizenship was legally expanded to the children of immigrants.

item.description: Hundreds of thousands of Indian professionals work and study in the country on H-1B visas, L-1 intra-company transfer visas, and F-1 student visas, while more than a million Indians remain in the employment-based green card backlog
item.content:encoded: Hundreds of thousands of Indian professionals work and study in the country on H-1B visas, L-1 intra-company transfer visas, and F-1 student visas, while more than a million Indians remain in the employment-based green card backlog

item.title: "Microsoft Powered Genocide": Employee Resigns, Sends Mass Email

media:content.media:description: Microsoft has rejected claims that its services are used to harm civilians.

item.description: A former Microsoft data centre technician in Italy has resigned and publicly accused the company of supporting Israeli military operations through its cloud infrastructure and European data centres.
item.content:encoded: A former Microsoft data centre technician in Italy has resigned and publicly accused the company of supporting Israeli military operations through its cloud infrastructure and European data centres.

item.title: 'Can Bring In Legislation': Trump On Supreme Court's Citizenship Ruling

media:content.media:description: The Supreme Court agreed in a majority opinion penned by Chief Justice John Roberts

item.description: In a post on X, US President Donald Trump wrote that the Congress should start working "today" to end the "expensive and unfair" birthright citizenship.
item.content:encoded: In a post on X, US President Donald Trump wrote that the Congress should start working "today" to end the "expensive and unfair" birthright citizenship.

item.title: 11-Year-Old Canadian Boy Dies Of Rabies After Waking Up With Bat On His Face

media:content.media:description: His parents agreed to share his story to raise awareness about rabies.

item.description: Because the boy had no apparent bite or scratch marks and appeared perfectly healthy, his family did not seek medical care.
item.content:encoded: Because the boy had no apparent bite or scratch marks and appeared perfectly healthy, his family did not seek medical care.

item.title: 25-Year-Old Hindu Priest 'Kidnapped', 'Tortured' For Ransom In Bangladesh

media:content.media:description: It was not immediately clear if Deuri was targeted because of his faith.

item.description: Deuri -- a first-year student at Central Law College in Old Dhaka, who also occasionally serves as an assistant priest at the central temple of Jagannath University -- was targeted by unidentified persons late on Monday.
item.content:encoded: Deuri -- a first-year student at Central Law College in Old Dhaka, who also occasionally serves as an assistant priest at the central temple of Jagannath University -- was targeted by unidentified persons late on Monday.

item.title: Airlines Baggage Mishandling Down By 23%, Still Costs $6.3 Billion: Report

media:content.media:description: Baggage mishandling rate fell 23 per cent to 4.9 per 1,000 passengers in 2025.

item.description: With net profit averaging just $8 per passenger, one mishandled bag wipes out the profit from more than 30 seats sold, the report said.
item.content:encoded: With net profit averaging just $8 per passenger, one mishandled bag wipes out the profit from more than 30 seats sold, the report said.

item.title: US Lifts Restrictions On Anthropic's Fable 5, Mythos 5 AI Models

media:content.media:description: Earlier this month, US authorities blocked access to the models on national security grounds.

item.description: Anthropic will soon begin restoring access globally to its most powerful AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, after the US government lifted a restriction on where they could be released, the company said Tuesday.
item.content:encoded: Anthropic will soon begin restoring access globally to its most powerful AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, after the US government lifted a restriction on where they could be released, the company said Tuesday.

item.title: Microsoft To Cut Under 2.5% Of Workforce In Latest Layoffs: Report

media:content.media:description: In July 2025, the company said it would lay off nearly 4% of its workforce.

item.description: Microsoft is planning to cut under 2.5% of its workforce in the latest round of layoffs that could be announced as early as next week, Business Insider reported on Tuesday, citing sources.
item.content:encoded: Microsoft is planning to cut under 2.5% of its workforce in the latest round of layoffs that could be announced as early as next week, Business Insider reported on Tuesday, citing sources.

item.title: 3-Year-Old Rescued From Collapsed Building 6 Days After Venezuela Quakes

media:content.media:description: A child receives emergency medical attention after being rescued by rescuers from rubble.

item.description: Venezuela was hit by two earthquakes of magnitudes 7.2 and 7.5 less than a minute apart last Wednesday, toppling buildings and trapping thousands of people beneath the rubble, according to authorities and rescue teams.
item.content:encoded: Venezuela was hit by two earthquakes of magnitudes 7.2 and 7.5 less than a minute apart last Wednesday, toppling buildings and trapping thousands of people beneath the rubble, according to authorities and rescue teams.

item.title: World Bank To Phase Out Lending To China By 2031

media:content.media:description: On June 16, the World Bank announced a similar plan for Poland, planning to reduce loans to zero by 2031.

item.description: The World Bank will phase out its lending to China by 2031, according to the organization's new country partnership framework, a source familiar with the matter told AFP on Tuesday.
item.content:encoded: The World Bank will phase out its lending to China by 2031, according to the organization's new country partnership framework, a source familiar with the matter told AFP on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Earned Nearly $1.2 Billion From Crypto Businesses Last Year

media:content.media:description: Trump got more than $500 million from his World Liberty Financial business selling new crypto products.

item.description: President Trump's income disclosure also mentions $635 million in royalties received under a licensing agreement related to the $TRUMP cryptocurrency.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's income disclosure also mentions $635 million in royalties received under a licensing agreement related to the $TRUMP cryptocurrency.

item.title: Boeing Says IT Outage Affected Computer Systems, Denies Cyberattack

media:content.media:description: The outage "significantly disrupted" the firm's commercial and military production, the Boeing said.

item.description: Boeing said on Tuesday that an unplanned IT outage affected some of its computer systems and applications.
item.content:encoded: Boeing said on Tuesday that an unplanned IT outage affected some of its computer systems and applications.

item.title: Inside Trump's Camp, Why JD Vance And Marco Rubio Differ On Iran, Lebanon

media:content.media:description: The experts believe that both JD Vance and Marco Rubio do not share the same worldview.

item.description: According to the Trump officials, Rubio was so skeptical of obtaining an acceptable deal with Iran that he declined to head the US delegation to the first ceasefire negotiations in April in Islamabad, Pakistan.
item.content:encoded: According to the Trump officials, Rubio was so skeptical of obtaining an acceptable deal with Iran that he declined to head the US delegation to the first ceasefire negotiations in April in Islamabad, Pakistan.

item.title: Kim Jong Un Vows To Deepen Ties With China In Message To Xi Jinping

media:content.media:description: Kim Jong Un described recent summit talks with Xi Jinping in Pyongyang as a "historic occasion".

item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un vowed on Wednesday to keep strengthening relations with China, describing recent summit talks with President Xi Jinping in Pyongyang as a "historic occasion".
item.content:encoded: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un vowed on Wednesday to keep strengthening relations with China, describing recent summit talks with President Xi Jinping in Pyongyang as a "historic occasion".

item.title: Russia Urges Pakistan, Afghanistan To Use Diplomacy To Solve Disputes

media:content.media:description: Pakistan accuses Afghanistan of harbouring militants it blames for plotting attacks in Pakistan.

item.description: Russia's Foreign Ministry urged Pakistan and Afghanistan on Tuesday to use diplomacy to resolve their longstanding differences.
item.content:encoded: Russia's Foreign Ministry urged Pakistan and Afghanistan on Tuesday to use diplomacy to resolve their longstanding differences.

item.title: Israel Ex-Army Chief Launches Campaign In Bid To Unseat Netanyahu

media:content.media:description: Israel's former army chief of staff Gadi Eisenkot on Tuesday announced the start of his poll campaign.

item.description: Four months ahead of the planned polls, Gadi Eisenkot held his first electoral meeting, where he declared that "Israel deserves to open a new chapter. We will write it together."
item.content:encoded: Four months ahead of the planned polls, Gadi Eisenkot held his first electoral meeting, where he declared that "Israel deserves to open a new chapter. We will write it together."

item.title: Ready For War If Dialogue Fails: Iran Warns US Ahead Of Qatar Talks
item.description: Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf on Tuesday said that Iran was prioritising diplomacy with the United States, but remained ready for war.
item.content:encoded: Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf on Tuesday said that Iran was prioritising diplomacy with the United States, but remained ready for war.

item.title: Israel Won't Leave Lebanon As long As Hezbollah "Threatens Us": Netanyahu

media:content.media:description: Netanyahu said that Israel will not leave southern Lebanon until the threat has disappeared.

item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu visited troops in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, vowing that his country's forces would stay in the area as long as Iran-backed Hezbollah remained a "threat".
item.content:encoded: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu visited troops in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, vowing that his country's forces would stay in the area as long as Iran-backed Hezbollah remained a "threat".

item.title: "Civilians Suffering, Children Dying": Ukraine's Drone War Reaches Moscow

media:content.media:description: Russia has not officially confirmed that the satellite centre was hit

item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said the facility plays a role in intelligence operations and helps coordinate Russian military activities in Ukraine.
item.content:encoded: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said the facility plays a role in intelligence operations and helps coordinate Russian military activities in Ukraine.

item.title: 300 Excess Deaths Recorded In France During May Heatwave
item.description: French health officials said on Tuesday that there were at least 300 more deaths than expected during a five-day heatwave in May, the country's first of the year.
item.content:encoded: French health officials said on Tuesday that there were at least 300 more deaths than expected during a five-day heatwave in May, the country's first of the year.
